text: - **Double-publishing / redundant heartbeats.** Two independent, uncoordinated ~30s timers both publish a fresh kind:30315 for the same session: the per-engine heartbeat via the outbox (`runtime.rs:276-289`, `TENEX_EDGE_HEARTBEAT_MS`, default 30s) and the daemon-wide `spawn_status_heartbeat_publisher` (`status_publish.rs:76-118`, `HEARTBEAT_SECS`=30s, `domain.rs:17`) which calls `set_status` directly, bypassing the outbox. Neither is aware of the other; a session can get two near-simultaneous, slightly different (different `now`/`expires_at`) 30315 events per ~30s window. [...] - **No dedup / no change-detection before publish.** Confirmed by grep — the only `dedup()` calls in these files are on the channel-list `Vec<String>` (`runtime.rs:133`, `status_publish.rs:44`, `provider.rs:268`), not on status content. Every heartbeat tick, turn-end, startup, and distill-applied event is republished even if `title`/`activity`/`busy` are byte-identical to the last publish.
